title: Meta Ads Fundamentals
description: Master advertising on Facebook and Instagram. Learn how to target specific demographics, create visually engaging ad creatives, set up the Meta Pixel, and launch campaigns that drive real business results.

seo_metadata.title: Meta Ads Fundamentals Certificate Course | ProCerix
seo_metadata.description: Learn Facebook and Instagram advertising. Master audience targeting, ad creatives, and the Meta Pixel for conversion tracking.

learning_outcomes: Navigate the Meta Business Manager and Ads Manager interfaces
Define campaign objectives (Awareness, Traffic, Conversions) based on business goals
Build highly targeted audiences (Core, Custom, and Lookalike)
Install and configure the Meta Pixel for conversion tracking
Design effective ad creatives tailored for the Facebook and Instagram feeds

lessons.title: Campaign Structure
lessons.content:
Campaign, Ad Set, Ad

Meta Ads are structured in three levels. The Campaign dictates the objective (e.g., Sales). The Ad Set dictates the audience, budget, and schedule. The Ad dictates the creative (image/video and text).

lessons.title: The Meta Pixel and CAPI
lessons.content:
Tracking the Journey

The Pixel is a piece of code on your website that tracks user actions (like 'Add to Cart'). The Conversions API (CAPI) sends data directly from your server to Meta, bypassing ad blockers.

lessons.title: Core and Custom Audiences
lessons.content:
Who Are You Reaching?

Core audiences are built using demographics and interests. Custom audiences are built using your own data, such as your email list or people who have visited your website (retargeting).

lessons.title: Lookalike Audiences
lessons.content:
Scaling Winners

A Lookalike Audience uses Meta's algorithm to find new people who have similar characteristics to your best existing customers (e.g., a 1% Lookalike of past purchasers).

lessons.title: Ad Creative Best Practices
lessons.content:
Stopping the Scroll

Your creative is the most important factor in a campaign's success. Use vertical video, capture attention in the first 3 seconds, and ensure your message is clear even without sound.

lessons.title: Testing and Scaling
lessons.content:
Finding What Works

Never rely on a single ad. Test multiple creatives against each other using Dynamic Creative Optimization (DCO). Once you find a winning ad, scale the budget slowly to maintain ROI.

mcqs.question: What are the three levels of the Meta Ads campaign structure?
At which level of the Meta Ads structure do you define your target audience and budget?
What is the primary purpose of the Meta Pixel?

options.content: To make images load faster on Facebook
To track website visitor actions and attribute conversions to your ads
To block competitors from seeing your website

mcqs.question: Which type of audience would you use if you want to show ads to people who visited your website but didn't purchase?

options.content: An audience of people who look physically similar to your CEO
An audience created by Meta's algorithm to find new people who share characteristics with a 'seed' audience (like your best customers)
An audience that only sees image ads
An audience made entirely of fake bot accounts

mcqs.question: When designing video ads for Instagram Stories or Reels, what aspect ratio is best practice?
In modern Meta advertising, which element has the biggest impact on campaign performance?

options.content: The time of day the ad launches

mcqs.question: What does CAPI stand for in the context of Meta tracking?
If your primary goal is to generate sales on your e-commerce store, which Campaign Objective should you choose?

options.content: A feature where Meta automatically edits your video to make it shorter
A feature where you provide multiple images, videos, headlines, and descriptions, and Meta automatically tests combinations to find what works best
A feature that forces your ad to only show on desktop computers
A bidding strategy that lowers costs
